Empires & Puzzles Eloise Review: The Ultimate Minion Counter?

Eloise was once the terror of all minion summoners, locking them out of their own game. But with stats from 2022, can she survive long enough to cast her spell in 2026? We analyze if her unique mechanics are worth the risk.
Eloise Review: The Minion Exorcist Who Forgot Her Armor
Eloise: The Minion Exorcist Who Forgot Her Armor
Eloise is a 5-star Legendary Holy hero from the

Alliance Quest. Originally released in early 2022, she was designed as the ultimate answer to the "Minion Meta" that dominated the game at the time (think Bera, Freya, and the Christmas family). She doesn't just clean up the mess; she puts a "Do Not Enter" sign on the enemy health bar.

While her mechanic remains unique and powerful, she is a classic hero living in a modern world. Bringing her into a raid today is like bringing a flintlock pistol to a laser fight—it still hurts if you hit, but you better be quick.

Eloise features a classic high-fantasy design, donning ornate blue and gold armor that fits the "Alliance Quest" aesthetic perfectly. She looks like a battle-hardened mage-knight ready to purge corruption. It's a refreshing, grounded look compared to the neon-soaked, particle-effect-heavy designs of 2026. She looks like she means business, even if her HP bar suggests she's made of fine porcelain.
Let's address the elephant in the room:
In the current meta of January 2026, where new heroes boast base stats exceeding 1300 Attack/Defense and 2200 Health, Eloise's base stats (~800/790/1390) are critically low.
Her "high" attack from 2022 is now lower than many modern 4-star heroes.

Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities

The Raven family bonus gives a chance to make Special Skill damage uncleansable. Honestly, this is largely irrelevant for Eloise since her primary function is minion removal, not applying debuffs (aside from the immunity, which is a status ailment). It's a nice-to-have, but don't build a team around it.
She has no innate passives. This is a hallmark of her age. While modern heroes have 3-4 paragraphs of passive text, Eloise just has her special skill.

Heals for a percentage of normal damage dealt. This is... okay. It helps her survivability slightly, but since her tile damage will be low compared to modern heroes, the healing isn't massive. Defense Up or Damage Reduction would have been far better for her.
Upon release, Eloise was a Tier-1 superstar. The "Minion Meta" was oppressive, and she was the key to unlocking defenses guarded by
. She was a staple in top-tier wars.
She has been crept hard. Her stats are obsolete, and her damage is negligible. However, her specific mechanic—

She is still a hard counter to annoying summoners, provided she isn't one-shot before charging.

If you pull her, you have a valuable tool. Even at 3/70, she can be used in Minion Wars to strip shields, though she will be incredibly fragile. Do not prioritize her over a modern stat monster, but keep her for those specific battles where you just
You likely have her on the bench. Is she worth the Alpha Aethers?

The stat boost from LB2 won't bring her up to modern standards. However, if you struggle specifically in Minion Wars, dusting her off and giving her some emblems (re-specced for defense) can give you a reliable 5th or 6th war team flag.
"She’s basically a Grimble that does not produce mana. Hits stronger... but Grimble charges the real killers."
— A common debate in 2022. Ironically, Grimble's mana boost has aged better than Eloise's damage.
"Not a fan of these new heroes... who make a whole subsection of heroes totally useless."
— A player predicting the binary nature of her design.
— A sentiment from 2022 that sadly no longer holds true. Now she's more of a wrecking ball made of papier-mâché.

Eloise is a relic of a different era, a specialized tool in a Swiss Army Knife world. She does one thing—exorcising minions—better than almost anyone else. If you are facing a wall of minions and need a silver bullet, she is your girl. Just make sure you bring a bodyguard, because she forgot to wear her armor to the battlefield.
Keep her for Minion Wars, but don't expect her to carry your team in daily raids.
Is Eloise still the queen of minion control in 2026? A detailed review of her stats, special skill, and viability in the modern meta.
